title: dryadella sublata
intro: Dryadella sublata, also known as the "orchid of the forest," is a type of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Dryadella sublata are said to include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dryadella sublata are often made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically as a poultice.
